A 20-year-old male was admitted in the hospital for complaints of bleeding from gums and petechiae of one-week duration. Patient developed sub conjunctival haemorrhage and malena on the first day of admission. Investigations done at the hospital showed - Hb: 8g/dl, Platelet count: 15,000/cu mm, Total leucocyte count: 1,20,000/cu mm, Bleeding time: 15 minutes, CT: >20 minutes, PT 40 seconds [control 12 seconds]. Peripheral blood smear showed predominantly large cells with high N/C ratio, fine chromatin, 1-2 nucleoli and abundant cytoplasmic granules. Auer rods were seen in many cells.
What is the probable diagnosis in this case? Give reasons. What is pathogenesis of bleeding in the above case? List any other four causes which can result in a similar condition. Mention one specific test and its finding for confirming the diagnosis of disease and also mention one test to confirm the complication as noted above case.(1+2+2+2=7)

A 55-year-old lady presented with postmenopausal bleeding for 10 days. On examination she was slightly obese. Pallor ++. Per abdominal examination revealed distention of abdomen and mild ascites +. Ascitic tap done was haemorrhagic. Ultrasound revealed left ovarian mass measuring 7x5x5 cm, solid and cystic in nature with yellowish brown areas. Endometrium appeared thickened, thickness was 20mm. Patient underwent hysterectomy with bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy. Section from left ovarian mass showed Call Exner bodies. i) What is your probable diagnosis? Give reasons. ii) What is the origin of the lesion in left ovary? iii) What is the pathological change that can occur in the endometrium in this condition? iv) What would be the clinical symptoms if this tumour occurs in young girls. v) Which hormone is produced in excess by this tumour? vi) Describe the histopathology of the lesion in left ovary. (2+1+1+1+1+3=9)
